ERBB2.summary: ERBB2 encodes a receptor tyrosine kinase whose amplification drives tumor proliferation and survival.

KRT7.summary: KRT7 is a luminal epithelial intermediate filament expressed in the vast majority of invasive breast carcinomas.

EPCAM.summary: EPCAM is a transmembrane glycoprotein driving epithelial proliferation, adhesion, and signaling.

FOXA1.summary: FOXA1 is a luminal hormone-sensing epithelial identity gene that anchors the luminal breast tumor transcriptional program.

CCND1.summary: CCND1 encodes Cyclin D1, a G1/S cell-cycle driver that is frequently amplified in breast tumor cells.

GATA3.summary: GATA3 is a transcription factor driving luminal epithelial differentiation and ER-positive identity in breast tumors.

KRT8.summary: KRT8 is a luminal epithelial cytoskeletal protein that reliably marks breast tumor cells (CK8/18/19-positive, ER-positive lineage).

TACSTD2.summary: TACSTD2 (TROP2) is an epithelial glycoprotein overexpressed in breast carcinoma cells, marking tumor identity.

SERHL2.summary: A serine hydrolase-like gene expressed in breast glandular tumor cells under hypoxia., Shamis et al.

ANKRD30A.summary: Breast-restricted mammary differentiation antigen (NY-BR-1) marking luminal tumor epithelium.

FASN.summary: FASN drives de novo lipogenesis, marking proliferating breast tumor epithelial cells.

MLPH.summary: MLPH links vesicle transport to myosin Va, marking luminal breast tumor glandular identity.

CDH1.summary: CDH1 encodes E-cadherin, the epithelial adhesion protein whose loss marks tumor invasion.

USP53.summary: Deubiquitinase that stabilizes CRKL, driving breast tumor growth and metastasis.

TMEM147.summary: ER-resident translocon subunit supporting multi-pass membrane protein biogenesis in breast tumor cells., McGilvray et al.

LARS.summary: LARS charges leucine onto tRNA, fueling tumor protein synthesis and proliferation.

ACTG2.summary: ACTG2 encodes gamma-smooth muscle actin, marking myoepithelial rather than tumor cells.

QARS.summary: QARS glutaminylates FBW7, stabilizing c-Myc and Mcl-1 to drive tumor proliferation., Wang M et al.

SEC11C.summary: SEC11C cleaves signal peptides in the ER, enabling secretory protein maturation in tumor cells.

SEC24A.summary: SEC24A is a COPII coat subunit that selects cargo for ER export.

VOPP1.summary: VOPP1 is a prosurvival oncoprotein that promotes tumor cell proliferation and suppresses apoptosis.
